title: The Drawer Beneath All Drawers

features.name: the signature in the margin
features.desc-on-touch: a hand-written note in the margin, decades old; it says only 'remember this'—and the drawer held whatever needed remembering

features.name: the filing row that knows itself
features.desc-on-touch: row after row of standing cards; each marks a smaller file, and the filing system has become its own archive

features.name: the drawer's own weight
features.desc-on-touch: layered wood and metal; it grows heavier with each opening, as though remembering adds mass to itself

features.name: the smallest compartment
features.desc-on-touch: a division in the drawer so small only fingers can reach; the memory inside has learned to take up no space

features.name: the label that rewrote itself
features.desc-on-touch: the handwritten label shifts with the light; you read a different word each time depending on how the beam enters

inscription: each drawer was smaller than the last; the memory chose smallness to stay unmissed

file.name: Archive_Containment_Study_1998.txt
file.note: proposal for nested storage; the theory holds that intimacy and invisibility are the same act

reverie.title: REVERIE — the drawer where two hands searched
narr: you lay on your side, half inside the smallest drawer,
your hand reaching the back where the photograph lived.
she knelt beside you, holding the drawer open wider.
the light was almost gone; you found the photograph
by touch alone. neither of you moved for a long time,
knowing the moment you closed it, it would be sealed

exits.label: up to the chamber where memory becomes immeasurable
deeper into compartments that have no names
toward where the archive stores its frozen moments
